Bambu Lab A1 mini.notes: Entry bedslinger with AMS lite support; high-speed capable.

Bambu Lab X1 Carbon.notes: Flagship consumer with LiDAR & Micro Lidar scan.

Bambu Lab X1E.notes: Engineering variant with heated chamber and ethernet only.

Biqu Hurakan.notes: First budget printer with stock Klipper firmware.

Creality Ender 3.notes: The printer that democratised FDM; starting point for millions.

Creality K1C.notes: CF-capable with stock hardened tri-metal nozzle.

Elegoo Centauri Carbon.notes: Enclosed CoreXY with hardened nozzle; CF-capable budget pick.

FLsun SR.notes: Fast delta for tall vases / cylindrical parts.

LulzBot TAZ 6.notes: Open-hardware workhorse; excellent for industrial filaments.

MakerBot Replicator 2.notes: PLA-only; no heated bed; the printer that broke into schools.

Prusa Core ONE.notes: Prusa's first consumer CoreXY; MK4-compatible hotend.

Prusa MK4S.notes: MK3 lineage with Nextruder; MMU3 optional.

Prusa MMU3 (MK4S host).notes: MMU3 upgrade to MK4S — 5-material single nozzle.

Prusa XL (5 head).notes: 5-tool toolchanger; segmented bed for active tool only.

Prusa i3 MK3S+.notes: The workhorse MK3 lineage; MMU2S/3 capable.

Qidi Q1 Pro.notes: Actively heated chamber at budget price — great for ASA/PA.

Raise3D Forge1.notes: Industrial-grade IDEX with soluble support capability.

RatRig V-Core 4 (300).notes: Redesigned CoreXY with Galileo 2 extruder support.

RepRap Darwin.notes: First widely-replicated RepRap; no heated bed.

RepRap Mendel90.notes: Classic self-replicating design; still a popular teaching build.

Sovol SV06.notes: Budget direct-drive with all-metal hotend; Mini+ alternative.

UltiMaker S7.notes: Auto-leveling; PEI sheet; soluble support via second head.

Voron Switchwire.notes: MK3-conversion Voron; belt-driven Z and X.

VzBoT 235.notes: Purpose-built for benchmark speeds; lightweight AWD toolhead.
